question: Design a `Book` class with private attributes for `title`, `author`, and `isbn`. Implement a public constructor to initialize these attributes and a `displayDetails()` method to print all book information.

test_cases.expected:
Book Details:
Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Author: Douglas Adams
ISBN: 978-0345391803
---
Book Details:
Title: Pride and Prejudice
Author: Jane Austen
ISBN: 978-0141439518
---
Book Details:
Title: Empty Title
Author: 
ISBN: No ISBN

errors: The constructor `Book(const char* t, const char* a, const char* i)` implicitly relies on `std::string(const char*)`. This constructor is vulnerable if `nullptr` is passed for any of the `char*` arguments, leading to undefined behavior or a crash. A robust implementation would explicitly check for `nullptr` before assignment or, preferably, use `std::string` parameters directly to leverage `std::string`'s safe construction.
